title: Marbella Catamaran Rental  2026 Costs and Guide
html:
Renting a catamaran in Marbella offers a relaxed way to explore the Costa del Sol coastline, with prices for a full-day crewed charter starting from around 2,500 EUR. Departures typically leave from Puerto José Banús, and you can compare options and book a catamaran through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Marbella?

A crewed catamaran for a full day generally costs between 2,500 and 5,000 EUR depending on size, season, and onboard extras. Prices are higher in July and August when demand peaks. A half-day charter on a smaller catamaran starts around 1,500 EUR. For those on a tighter budget, shared group tours on a catamaran can cost as little as 150 EUR per person. You can compare verified boat listings and book a boat online for the best available prices.

Which catamaran should I rent in Marbella?

Choosing the right catamaran depends on your group size and preferences. The following list outlines popular options available for hire.

  1. Lagoon 450, best for families or two couples, sleeps up to 8, from 3,000 EUR per day.
  2. Lagoon 620, ideal for larger groups, accommodates 12 guests, from 4,500 EUR per day.
  3. Bali 4.1, a modern catamaran with spacious deck areas, sleeps 8, from 2,800 EUR per day.
  4. Nautitech 44, a sportier option for keen sailors, capacity 6, from 2,200 EUR per day.
  5. Power catamaran (e.g. Privilege 585), offers motor cruising speed, carries up to 12, from 3,500 EUR per day.

Motor yacht enthusiasts might prefer the Princess V58 for hire, a 58-foot yacht with a flybridge.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most catamaran rentals depart from Puerto José Banús, the glamorous marina in the heart of Marbella’s Golden Mile. Other popular departure points include the Puerto Deportivo de Marbella (the main marina near the old town) and Cabopino Marina, a smaller harbour east of the city. From these marinas, typical routes head west towards the coast of Estepona or east to the nature reserve of the Dunas de Artola. The bay of Marbella itself offers calm waters and stunning views of the Sierra Blanca mountains. A common day trip covers a round trip of about 25 nautical miles, including a stop for swimming at a secluded cove.

What are the peak seasons and sailing conditions?

The high season for catamaran rental in Marbella runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest months. The prevailing wind is the Poniente, a westerly breeze that usually provides pleasant sailing conditions in the morning before strengthening in the afternoon. The Levante wind from the east can bring more chop but is less common in summer. Always check the local forecast before setting off.

For the calmest sailing and best visibility, book a morning departure between 10 am and 12 pm, when the sea is often glassy and the wind is light.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Marbella?

For bareboat catamaran charter, a valid International Certificate of Competence or equivalent is legally required in Spain. However, most catamaran rentals in Marbella are offered with a professional skipper included, so no licence is needed if you book a crewed charter. The operator will confirm the exact requirements based on the boat and your qualifications. To understand the booking process, see how boat rental works on the platform.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,500, 5,000 EUR per day (crewed catamaran)
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Typical trip length: Full-day (8 hours) or half-day (4 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Typical distance: 25 nautical miles round trip

Questions about catamaran rental in Marbella

Can I rent a catamaran for a group of 12 people in Marbella?

Yes, many catamarans such as the Lagoon 620 or Bali 4.1 are licensed for up to 12 guests and are available with a skipper for a full-day charter.

Is it possible to go on a half-day catamaran trip in Marbella?

Yes, half-day charters of around 4 hours are widely offered, often departing at 10am or 2pm, at prices starting from about 1,500 EUR.

What is included in the catamaran rental price in Marbella?

Most crewed charters include the skipper, fuel, insurance, and standard safety equipment; food and drinks are often extra but can be pre-ordered.

Are there sunset catamaran cruises available from Marbella?

Yes, many operators offer sunset cruises lasting about 2, 3 hours, leaving around 6pm in summer, with prices from 800 EUR for a private group.

Whether you choose a catamaran or another boat, BoatHire24 connects you with trusted operators for a day on the water in Marbella. Owners can also list their own boats to reach a global audience, with no booking fees until you confirm a rental. To browse all options, check availability and find the perfect vessel for your 2026 adventure.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who list and book verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
